creator: zirias@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Dave Hayes from comment #13)
> BTW I imported a -tagged- developer release. If I understand this correctly, a
> git tag doesn't move. Also, if you look at the repo, this was tagged 10 months
> ago and hasn't moved.
It's technically possible to move a tag, but it *should* not move, so this wasn't the point. I called it "moving" because this project seems to publish multiple "revisions" of the same developer release. To do it correctly, you'd have to include this revision number as well in DISTVERSION.

But my main concern was indeed just the fact that this is labelled a *developer* release. It's certainly ok to use that for a port, but there should be a reason to prefer it over the latest *stable* release. I'd rather avoid such decisions on ports I don't maintain myself, unless I can present a strong reason...
